summary: The agent concludes that ivosidenib has not been FDA-approved for acute myeloid leukemia because none of the cited sources confirm its approval for AML, even though it is available as an option for IDH1-mutated AML cases alongside olutasidenib, which was noted to receive FDA approval. Despite clinical relevance in treating mutant-IDH1-positive AML, lack of explicit FDA authorization makes the affirmative response invalid within the context of these documented facts.
